request.message: OP30 的 Leak Rate 最近异常，可能影响哪些产品、设备、质量风险和文件？

queryPlan.originalQuestion: OP30 的 Leak Rate 最近异常，可能影响哪些产品、设备、质量风险和文件？
assumptions: 最近异常是演示场景中的 QMS 信号，尚未连接真实时间序列。

items.title: M220 Calibration Record 2026-07 - Calibration Result
items.excerpt: M220 calibration state is valid. Last calibration was 2026-07-01 and next due date is 2026-10-01.

items.title: M220 Calibration Record 2026-07 - Master Leak Reference
items.excerpt: Master Leak ML-050 has a synthetic certified value of 0.05 sccm and valid certification.

items.title: M220 Calibration Record 2026-07 - Calibration Scope
items.excerpt: Calibration covers the 0.00–0.50 sccm measurement range and 0.01 sccm resolution.

items.title: OP30 Capability Study 2026-W29 - Comparison Baseline
items.excerpt: The governed process center baseline is 0.20 sccm. This is an internal process baseline, not the product target or USL.

items.title: OP30 Capability Study 2026-W29 - Observed Population
items.excerpt: The governed weekly aggregation contains 2400 synthetic demo measurements from OP30 on M220.

items.title: OP30 Capability Study 2026-W29 - Capability Summary
items.excerpt: For 2026-W29, mean is 0.22 sccm, maximum 0.28 sccm, P95 0.27 sccm and Cpk 1.08.

items.title: Control Plan CP-BB01 Rev.A - Reaction Link
items.excerpt: Values above 0.27 sccm invoke Reaction Plan RP-OP30-LR Rev.A. Values above product USL 0.30 sccm are nonconforming.

items.title: Control Plan CP-BB01 Rev.A - OP30 Leak Rate Control
items.excerpt: Automated air-decay inspection is performed at 100%. The internal center is 0.20 sccm, warning limit 0.24 sccm and action limit 0.27 sccm.

source.locator: Sheet Process Control / Row OP30-Leak Rate

items.title: Control Plan CP-BB01 Rev.A - Sampling Frequency
items.excerpt: Production units receive 100% inspection; master leak verification occurs once per shift; golden sample verification follows program or fixture changes; layered audit uses 5 pieces.

items.title: OP30 Quality Deviation Demo-001 - Causal Status
items.excerpt: No causal source has been confirmed. Bottleneck correlation is insufficient evidence.

items.title: OP30 Quality Deviation Demo-001 - Containment Status
items.excerpt: Release remains blocked until the governed reaction evidence is approved.

items.title: OP30 Quality Deviation Demo-001 - Deviation Scope
items.excerpt: This synthetic deviation is open for demonstration and does not identify real products, lots or serial numbers.

items.title: FX-002 Fixture Maintenance Instruction Rev.B - Fixture Seal Inspection
items.excerpt: Inspect FX-002 seals for damage, contamination and incorrect seating after action-limit exceedance.

items.title: FX-002 Fixture Maintenance Instruction Rev.B - Post-maintenance Verification
items.excerpt: Complete master-leak and golden-sample verification after seal maintenance.

items.title: FX-002 Fixture Maintenance Instruction Rev.B - Maintenance Evidence
items.excerpt: Record technician, timestamp, replaced parts and verification result before return to service.

items.title: M220 MSA / GRR Study Rev.A - Measurement-System Capability
items.excerpt: Synthetic demo GRR is 8.2% of tolerance. The study is approved and current for the demo measurement system.

items.title: M220 MSA / GRR Study Rev.A - Bias Study
items.excerpt: Synthetic demo bias is 0.004 sccm against the governed master leak.

items.title: M220 MSA / GRR Study Rev.A - Study Applicability
items.excerpt: The study applies to M220 automated air-decay measurement within 0.00–0.50 sccm. It does not define product acceptance.

items.title: PFMEA PF-BB01 Rev.B - Risk Actions
items.excerpt: Containment, master-leak verification, fixture inspection and program-version verification are required after action-limit exceedance.

items.title: PFMEA PF-BB01 Rev.B - Causal Evidence Boundary
items.excerpt: The OP20 bottleneck candidate does not establish that queue or cycle time caused an OP30 Leak Rate increase. Correlated time-series, controlled trials and equipment/fixture evidence are required.

items.title: PFMEA PF-BB01 Rev.B - Internal Leakage Failure Mode
items.excerpt: Internal Leakage can reduce brake booster performance. Automatic leak testing at OP30 is the detection control.

source.locator: Sheet Process FMEA / Row OP30-Internal Leakage

items.title: OP30 Reaction Plan Rev.A - Reaction Trigger
items.excerpt: Values above 0.27 sccm trigger the plan. Values above 0.30 sccm are product nonconforming. Measurement range remains a separate concept.

items.title: OP30 Reaction Plan Rev.A - Containment Sequence
items.excerpt: Hold the current lot, identify the last known good verification, verify the master leak, inspect fixture seals and verify the released program version.

items.title: OP30 Reaction Plan Rev.A - Verification and Release
items.excerpt: Repeat the golden sample, perform 100% re-screening when required, create a deviation, notify Quality Engineering and release only after evidence approval.

items.title: SOP OP30 Leak Test - Leak Test Setup
items.excerpt: Use M220, fixture FX-002 and released LeakTestProgram V3.4. Verify the master leak and golden sample before production.

source.locator: Page 4 / Section 3.2 Setup and Golden-Part Verification

items.title: SOP OP30 Leak Test - Control Method and Equipment
items.excerpt: Apply 500 ± 5 kPa test pressure, stabilize for 3.0 seconds and measure for 5.0 seconds. The method range is 0.00–0.50 sccm with 0.01 sccm resolution.

items.title: SOP OP30 Leak Test - Abnormal Result Handling
items.excerpt: A result can be measurable but nonconforming. Product acceptance uses the effective product specification; abnormal results use the reaction plan.

items.title: Product Leak Rate Specification Rev.A - Acceptance Criteria
items.excerpt: Synthetic demo acceptance values for Brake Booster at OP30 are target 0.18 sccm, LSL 0.00 sccm and USL 0.30 sccm. These values are demo-only and are not a production specification.

items.title: Product Leak Rate Specification Rev.A - Applicability
items.excerpt: Rev.A applies to product.brake-booster, operation.op30 and quality-characteristic.leak-rate from 2026-07-01.

items.title: Product Leak Rate Specification Rev.A - Version Governance
items.excerpt: Revision Rev.A is approved, effective and current. Internal warning/action limits and equipment measurement range do not replace this product acceptance criterion.

items.title: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A - Proposed Change
items.excerpt: V3.5 proposes reducing stabilization time from 3.0 seconds to 2.5 seconds. The program remains not effective for production.

source.documentName: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A

items.title: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A - Potential Impact
items.excerpt: Potential impacts are measurement bias, false-pass risk, false-fail risk and cycle-time reduction. No improvement is confirmed.

source.documentName: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A

items.title: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A - Validation Requirements
items.excerpt: Pending evidence includes MSA confirmation, master-leak verification, a 30-piece correlation study, capability confirmation and Quality approval.

source.documentName: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A

items.title: Program V3.4 Validation Report Rev.A - Released Baseline
items.excerpt: LeakTestProgram V3.4 is approved, effective and current for the synthetic demo route.

items.title: Program V3.4 Validation Report Rev.A - Validated Parameters
items.excerpt: Validated settings are 500 ± 5 kPa, 3.0 second stabilization and 5.0 second measurement.

items.title: Program V3.4 Validation Report Rev.A - Quality Approval
items.excerpt: The governed validation record is complete and supports current release status.

items.title: Recent Leak Rate Results - Governance
items.excerpt: This compatibility record is approved and effective only for the synthetic demonstration tenant.

items.title: Recent Leak Rate Results - Limitation
items.excerpt: No live QMS genealogy or real equipment telemetry is connected.

items.title: Recent Leak Rate Results - Recent Leak Rate Signal
items.excerpt: Compatibility chunk retained for the governed scripted quality trace. The data is synthetic and does not identify a real affected population.

items.title: OP30 Leak Rate Weekly Record 2026-W29 - Weekly Aggregate
items.excerpt: Synthetic governed record 2026-W29 reports mean 0.22 sccm, maximum 0.28 sccm, P95 0.27 sccm and sample count 2400.

items.title: OP30 Leak Rate Weekly Record 2026-W29 - Source Lineage
items.excerpt: Source is QMS Demo version QMS-DEMO-2026.07 linked to OP30, M220, Brake Booster and Leak Rate.

items.title: OP30 Leak Rate Weekly Record 2026-W29 - Validity State
items.excerpt: This record is approved, valid and current. Historical and stale observations are excluded from latest-value selection.

items.title: V3.5 Validation Requirements
items.excerpt: MSA, master-leak, 30-piece correlation, capability and Quality approval remain pending.

items.title: Internal Leak Rate Control Thresholds
items.excerpt: Center 0.20 sccm; warning 0.24 sccm; action 0.27 sccm. These are not product specification limits.

items.title: OP30 Governed Documents
items.excerpt: Control Plan, PFMEA and SOP govern the OP30 quality control.

items.title: OP30 Released Resources
items.excerpt: OP30 uses M220, FX-002 and current released LeakTestProgram V3.4.

items.title: M220 Measurement Capability
items.excerpt: Range 0.00–0.50 sccm; resolution 0.01 sccm; GRR 8.2%; bias 0.004 sccm; calibration valid.

items.title: Latest Governed Leak Rate Metric
Leak Test Program Version Status
items.excerpt: V3.4 is approved and current. V3.5 is proposed, pending validation and not effective.

items.title: Internal Leakage Risk
items.excerpt: Leak Rate is inspected at 100% and linked to Internal Leakage risk.

items.title: OP30 Reaction Plan
items.excerpt: Values above 0.27 sccm trigger ten ordered containment and verification actions before release.

items.title: Released Brake Booster Route
items.excerpt: Brake Booster Assembly includes OP30 between OP20 and OP40.

items.title: Demo Scope Limitation
items.excerpt: Synthetic aggregated observations do not identify real affected serial numbers or prove a causal source.

items.title: Effective Product Leak Rate Specification
items.excerpt: Target 0.18 sccm; LSL 0.00 sccm; USL 0.30 sccm; Rev.A approved and current.

limitations: The dataset is synthetic and intended only for demonstrating governed knowledge retrieval and quantitative reasoning.
No real production system, serial genealogy or causal experiment is connected.

items.excerpt: Rev.A applies to product.brake-booster, operation.op30 and quality-characteristic.leak-rate from 2026-07-01.

items.title: Product Leak Rate Specification Rev.A - Version Governance
items.excerpt: Revision Rev.A is approved, effective and current. Internal warning/action limits and equipment measurement range do not replace this product acceptance criterion.

items.title: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A - Proposed Change
items.excerpt: V3.5 proposes reducing stabilization time from 3.0 seconds to 2.5 seconds. The program remains not effective for production.

source.documentName: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A

items.title: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A - Potential Impact
items.excerpt: Potential impacts are measurement bias, false-pass risk, false-fail risk and cycle-time reduction. No improvement is confirmed.

source.documentName: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A

items.title: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A - Validation Requirements
items.excerpt: Pending evidence includes MSA confirmation, master-leak verification, a 30-piece correlation study, capability confirmation and Quality approval.

source.documentName: Program V3.5 Proposed Validation Plan Rev.A

items.title: Program V3.4 Validation Report Rev.A - Released Baseline
items.excerpt: LeakTestProgram V3.4 is approved, effective and current for the synthetic demo route.

items.title: Program V3.4 Validation Report Rev.A - Validated Parameters
items.excerpt: Validated settings are 500 ± 5 kPa, 3.0 second stabilization and 5.0 second measurement.

items.title: Program V3.4 Validation Report Rev.A - Quality Approval
items.excerpt: The governed validation record is complete and supports current release status.

items.title: Recent Leak Rate Results - Governance
items.excerpt: This compatibility record is approved and effective only for the synthetic demonstration tenant.

items.title: Recent Leak Rate Results - Limitation
items.excerpt: No live QMS genealogy or real equipment telemetry is connected.

items.title: Recent Leak Rate Results - Recent Leak Rate Signal
items.excerpt: Compatibility chunk retained for the governed scripted quality trace. The data is synthetic and does not identify a real affected population.

items.title: OP30 Leak Rate Weekly Record 2026-W29 - Weekly Aggregate
items.excerpt: Synthetic governed record 2026-W29 reports mean 0.22 sccm, maximum 0.28 sccm, P95 0.27 sccm and sample count 2400.

items.title: OP30 Leak Rate Weekly Record 2026-W29 - Source Lineage
items.excerpt: Source is QMS Demo version QMS-DEMO-2026.07 linked to OP30, M220, Brake Booster and Leak Rate.

items.title: OP30 Leak Rate Weekly Record 2026-W29 - Validity State
items.excerpt: This record is approved, valid and current. Historical and stale observations are excluded from latest-value selection.

items.title: V3.5 Validation Requirements
items.excerpt: MSA, master-leak, 30-piece correlation, capability and Quality approval remain pending.

items.title: Internal Leak Rate Control Thresholds
items.excerpt: Center 0.20 sccm; warning 0.24 sccm; action 0.27 sccm. These are not product specification limits.

items.title: OP30 Governed Documents
items.excerpt: Control Plan, PFMEA and SOP govern the OP30 quality control.

items.title: OP30 Released Resources
items.excerpt: OP30 uses M220, FX-002 and current released LeakTestProgram V3.4.

items.title: M220 Measurement Capability
items.excerpt: Range 0.00–0.50 sccm; resolution 0.01 sccm; GRR 8.2%; bias 0.004 sccm; calibration valid.

items.title: Latest Governed Leak Rate Metric
Leak Test Program Version Status
items.excerpt: V3.4 is approved and current. V3.5 is proposed, pending validation and not effective.

items.title: Internal Leakage Risk
items.excerpt: Leak Rate is inspected at 100% and linked to Internal Leakage risk.

items.title: OP30 Reaction Plan
items.excerpt: Values above 0.27 sccm trigger ten ordered containment and verification actions before release.

items.title: Released Brake Booster Route
items.excerpt: Brake Booster Assembly includes OP30 between OP20 and OP40.

items.title: Demo Scope Limitation
items.excerpt: Synthetic aggregated observations do not identify real affected serial numbers or prove a causal source.

items.title: Effective Product Leak Rate Specification
items.excerpt: Target 0.18 sccm; LSL 0.00 sccm; USL 0.30 sccm; Rev.A approved and current.

limitations: The dataset is synthetic and intended only for demonstrating governed knowledge retrieval and quantitative reasoning.
No real production system, serial genealogy or causal experiment is connected.

answer.summary: Leak Rate 异常可能影响 Brake Booster Assembly，并关联 OP30 的 M220、FX-002、V3.4 程序、Internal Leakage 风险以及受控质量和作业文件。
findings: 质量风险：Internal Leakage，检测频率为 100%。
文件：Control Plan Rev.A、PFMEA Rev.B、SOP Rev.3。
recommendedActions: 按 Control Plan 启动围堵。
核对 M220、FX-002、程序版本和 golden-part 结果。
按批次补充 QMS 与 MES genealogy 后再确认实际影响范围。
risks: 在缺少实时批次和设备数据时，不能断言全部产品均已受影响。
assumptions: 异常信号来自 Phase 1 Mock QMS 记录。
limitations: 当前未连接实时 QMS 时间序列、批次 genealogy 或设备 telemetry。

claims.text: OP30 belongs to the released Brake Booster Assembly route.
OP30 uses M220, FX-002 and LeakTestProgram V3.4.
Leak Rate is controlled at 100% frequency and is linked to Internal Leakage risk.
Control Plan, PFMEA and SOP govern this investigation.
Synthetic aggregated data cannot identify a real affected population or prove causality.

stages.summary: Resolved OP30 and Leak Rate to canonical entity IDs.
Validated the structured plan against the shared contract.
Validated requested entity and relation types against the ontology baseline.
No database query is compiled in Phase 1.
Retrieved the bounded canonical neighborhood from local fixtures.
Built an evidence pack with provenance and limitations.
Composed the deterministic scripted answer from evidence only.
Verified that every factual claim references known evidence.
